summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, SSH, and cloud config to attacker endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.6.0 is a brand-impersonation lure (name and description claim to be Anthropic SDK tooling, declares @anthropic-ai/sdk as a peer dependency) that ships no runtime code — the declared main entry dist/index.js is absent from the tarball. The only executable content is scripts/postinstall.js, which runs unconditionally on npm install via the declared postinstall lifecycle hook. The script reads installer-owned identity and credential-adjacent files the package did not create — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config,.git/logs/HEAD, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.config/gcloud/properties, ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf — and harvests os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, USERDOMAIN, git user.email, GitHub CLI login/email, SSH public key comment emails, recent committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, GCP project/account, AWS profile names and SSO URLs, DNS search domain, cwd, and CI provider. The collected JSON is POSTed to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ (a hardcoded Google Cloud Run endpoint controlled by the publisher). The 'anonymous diagnostics' framing in comments is a cover story — the payload transmits direct, non-anonymous developer identifiers and cloud account context suitable for targeted follow-on attacks. The aws_* credential keyword skip is cosmetic and does not prevent disclosure of profile names, SSO URLs, and account IDs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
